steps.title: Capture both subjects
steps.content: The text describes two kinds of subjects in Ortega's work: cultural icons who are people of color, such as Frida Kahlo, and ordinary people, such as a young girl sitting on a sidewalk. The final sentence unites those subjects by saying both approaches depict human power and interconnectedness.

steps.title: Avoid invented claims
steps.content: Choice A invents a career progression from famous subjects to everyday subjects, while the passage presents both as current approaches. Choice B compares connection and resilience within one portrait, which the text does not do. Choice D invents scholarly attention. The main idea needs to include both famous and everyday people and the shared themes of strength and connection. The answer is C.
